<!-- question-type: inclass -->
### Exercise 6: Loading and Inspecting Some Data

(a) Open the Posit Cloud Workspace for this workshop.

(b) Load the dataset by running this line of code:

```{r}
#| eval: false
library(readr)

inventory <- read_csv("data/inventory.csv") 
```

**(c)** We want to explain the code in (b) is doing, line by line.

* What does ` ```{r} ` mean in Quarto?
* What does `#| eval: false` do? 
* Do you need to change the `#| eval: false` setting to run the code when you render the file?
* What does `library(readr)` do?
* Explain what the assignment operator `<-`?, does.
* Is `read_csv()` a function or a variable?
* What does `"data/inventory.csv"` refer to?

(d)  Run each of the following commands, and explain the output to your group:

* `class(inventory)`
* `names(inventory)`
* `head(inventory)`
* `nrow(inventory)`
* `ncol(inventory)`


<!-- BEGIN PROFILE:r-teaching-guide -->
::: {.content-visible when-profile="r-teaching-guide"}

::: {.teaching-block}

::: {.teaching-block-header}
Teaching Note
:::

::: {.teaching-block-body}

🎯 **Learning Objective** 
Students should:

Interpret a Quarto R code chunk by explaining each line in plain English.

Distinguish what Quarto controls (eval) versus what R controls (library(), <-, functions).

Execute a data import step successfully by ensuring the chunk evaluates during render.

Use the “inspect ritual” (class, names, head, nrow, ncol) to verify what they imported.

Communicate what the inspection output implies about the dataset (type, variables, size, first rows).

✅   **Core Concepts to Highlight**

Code chunk (` ```{r} `): a runnable block of R code inside a `.qmd`.

`#| eval`: Quarto option controlling whether a chunk runs during rendering.

Package name + `library()`: loads a package so its functions are available.

Assignment `<-`: stores a value in an object name.

Function: takes inputs and returns an output (e.g., `read_csv()` reads a file).

Relative file path: "data/inventory.csv" means “look inside the data folder in this project for a file named ... .”

💬 **Suggested In-Class Prompts** (if needed)

“What’s the difference between running a chunk and rendering the document?”

“If eval: false, what do you expect to happen to inventory after render?”

“What does the left side of <- become?”

“What would you check first if the file can’t be found?”

📌 **Common Misunderstandings**

They think code in the console ‘counts’ for the report.

* Fix: “If it’s not in a chunk that evaluates, it won’t be reproduced when you render.”

They don’t realise eval: false prevents the dataset loading on render.

* Fix: make them predict the outcome: “Will inventory exist after render?”

They confuse `read_csv` vs "data/inventory.csv".

* Fix: “Function name vs input argument (file path).”

File path errors (data/inventory.csv not found).

* Fix: check the Files pane: does the data folder exist? is the file name exact?

They misinterpret `class()` output.

* Fix: accept both; emphasise “it’s a table-like object with rows/columns.”

:::

:::

:::
<!-- END PROFILE:r-teaching-guide -->

<!-- BEGIN PROFILE:r-solutions -->
::: {.content-visible when-profile="r-solutions" when-profile="r-teaching-guide"}

::: {.solution-block}

::: {.solution-block-header}
Solution
:::

::: {.solution-block-body}

**(c) Explaining the code chunk line by line**

- **What does ` ```{r} ` mean in Quarto?**  
  It starts an **R code chunk**. Code inside the chunk can be run and its output can appear in the rendered document.

- **What does `#| eval: false` do?**  
  It tells Quarto **not to run** this code chunk when rendering (it will be shown, but not executed).

- **Do you need to change the `#| eval: false` setting to run the code when you render the file?**  
  **Yes.** To load the dataset during rendering, you must remove it or change it (e.g., to `#| eval: true`) so the chunk executes.

- **What does `library(readr)` do?**  
  It loads the **readr** package so you can use functions from it (including `read_csv()`).

- **Explain what the assignment operator `<-` does.**  
  It **stores** the value on the right-hand side into the object name on the left-hand side.  
  Example: `inventory <- ...` creates an object called `inventory`.

- **Is `read_csv()` a function or a variable?**  
  It is a **function** (a command that takes inputs and returns an output).

- **What does `"data/inventory.csv"` refer to?**  
  It is the **file path** to the CSV inside the project.  
  `data/` is a folder; `inventory.csv` is the file name. It is **relative** to the project directory.

**(d) Inspect commands (what they return / how to interpret them)**

- **`class(inventory)`**  
  Tells you the **type of object** `inventory` is (typically something like a tibble/data frame).

- **`names(inventory)`**  
  Lists the **column names** (variable names) in the dataset.

- **`head(inventory)`**  
  Shows the **first few rows** so you can sanity-check that the data imported correctly.

- **`nrow(inventory)`**  
  Returns the **number of rows** (how many observations).

- **`ncol(inventory)`**  
  Returns the **number of columns** (how many variables).
:::

:::

:::
<!-- END PROFILE:r-solutions -->
